335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
420	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
421	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
422	
423	end Module
424	
425	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
426	
427	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
428	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
429	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
430	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
431	
432	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
433	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
434	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
435	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
436	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
437	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
438	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
439	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
440	
441	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
442	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
443	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
444	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
445	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
446	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
447	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
448	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
449	
450	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
451	
452	open CategoryTheory Limits
453	
454	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
455	
456	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
457	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
458	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
459	
460	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
461	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
462	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
463	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
464	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
465	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
466	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
467	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
468	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
469	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
470	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
471	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
472	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
473	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
474	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
475	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
476	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
477	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
478	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
479	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
480	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
481	  rw [key] at hinst0
482	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
483	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
484	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
485	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
486	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
487	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
488	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
489	  rw [he] at h2
490	  exact h2
491	
492	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
493	
494	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
495	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
496	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
497	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
498	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
499	
500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	/-- A morphism of sheaves of `R`-modules on `Spec R` that is an isomorphism on every basic open
549	`D(f)` is an isomorphism. This is the "isomorphism on a basis ⟹ isomorphism" reduction specialised
550	to the basic-open basis of `Spec R` (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`): injectivity on stalks is
551	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`, surjectivity on stalks is the basic-open germ lift, and
552	`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` concludes. Project-local glue used to assemble `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`
553	from per-basic-open section data. -/
554	private theorem isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
555	    {F G : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} R) (Spec R)} (α : F ⟶ G)
556	    (h : ∀ f : R, IsIso (α.1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) : IsIso α := by
557	  have hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _)) :=
558	    PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
559	  have hinj : ∀ U ∈ Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _),
560	      Function.Injective (α.1.app (.op U)) := by
561	    rintro U ⟨f, rfl⟩
562	    exact ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).1
563	  have hstalk : ∀ x, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor (ModuleCat.{u} R) x).map α.1) := by
564	    intro x
565	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
566	    refine ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB hinj x, ?_⟩
567	    intro t
568	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hUB, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB G.presheaf x t
569	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := hUB
570	    obtain ⟨s', rfl⟩ := ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).2 s
571	    exact ⟨F.presheaf.germ _ x hxU s', by rw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]⟩
572	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso α
573	
574	/-- A linear map intertwining two localizations of the same module at the same submonoid is
575	bijective: if `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : M →ₗ M''` both exhibit a localization at `S` and
576	`h : M' →ₗ M''` satisfies `h ∘ₗ f = g`, then `h` is bijective (it is the canonical localization
577	isomorphism `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`). Stated with the two `IsLocalizedModule` facts as
578	explicit hypotheses to avoid typeclass-diamond ambiguity at the call site. -/
579	private theorem bijective_comp_of_localizations {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
580	    {M M' M'' : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
581	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module R M''] {f : M →ₗ[R] M'} {g : M →ₗ[R] M''} {h : M' →ₗ[R] M''}
582	    (hf : IsLocalizedModule S f) (hg : IsLocalizedModule S g) (hh : h ∘ₗ f = g) :
583	    Function.Bijective h := by
584	  haveI := hf; haveI := hg
585	  have heq : h = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).toLinearMap := by
586	    apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext S (f := f) (f' := g)
587	    apply LinearMap.ext
588	    intro x
589	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, ← LinearMap.comp_apply, hh,
590	      LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
591	  rw [heq]
592	  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).bijective
593	
594	/-- **`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` from per-basic-open section localization** (the cheap stalk/section
595	assembly of `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the blueprint "G1-assemble" step). If for every
596	`f : R` the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` of a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R`
597	exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R` — exactly the conclusion of G1-core
598	(`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`,
599	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
600	an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).
601	
602	On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
603	two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
604	instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
605	localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
606	the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
607	`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
608	`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.
609	
610	This turns the remaining keystone obligation into exactly G1-core: combined with the converse engine
611	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is
612	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. Project-local: Mathlib has no `IsQuasicoherent → IsIso
613	fromTildeΓ` bridge. -/
614	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
615	    (M : (Spec R).Modules)
616	    (H : ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
617	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
618	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom) :
619	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
620	  haveI hmain : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ) := by
621	    apply isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen
622	    intro f
623	    set N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) with hN
624	    -- target localizes by hypothesis; source localizes by the `tilde.toOpen` instance
625	    haveI htgt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
626	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
627	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := H f
628	    set comp := (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))
629	      with hcomp
630	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
631	    have hcompose := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
632	    -- the component intertwines the two localization maps of `N` at `powers f`
633	    have h1 : comp.hom ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom
634	        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
635	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
636	      have e := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcompose
637	      rwa [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at e
638	    change Function.Bijective (⇑comp.hom)
639	    exact bijective_comp_of_localizations (Submonoid.powers f)
640	      (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule _ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom)
641	      (H f) h1
642	  exact SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.isIso_of_isIso_map M.fromTildeΓ
643	
644	/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
645	`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
646	section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
647	
648	The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
649	reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
650	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
651	formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
652	(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
653	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
654	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
655	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
656	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
657	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
658	  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,
659	    isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M⟩
660	
661	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1-core (Route F) building blocks
662	
663	The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
664	`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
665	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` this is
666	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — i.e. to the statement that a quasi-coherent sheaf on an
667	affine scheme lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence). That
668	equivalence is a genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit (Tilde.lean only proves the *globally
669	presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
670	
671	The composition lemma below discharges the **globally-presented** sub-case end to end (it is the
672	Route-F endpoint once a global presentation/tilde identification is in hand). The residual gap is
673	exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
674	see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
675	
676	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
677	admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
678	`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
679	
680	This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
681	forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
682	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
683	globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
684	presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.
685	Project-local. -/
686	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
687	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (_P : M.Presentation) (f : R) :
688	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
689	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
690	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
691	  haveI : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation M _P
692	  exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f
693	
694	/-- **`map_units` field of G1-core (Route F), for any sheaf of modules.** For `M : (Spec R).Modules`
695	and `f : R`, every element of `Submonoid.powers f` acts invertibly on the sections `Γ(M, D(f))` over
696	`R`. This is exactly the first field `IsLocalizedModule.map_units` of the target
697	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, in the shape the 3-field constructor consumes.
698	
699	It holds for an *arbitrary* `M` (no quasi-coherence needed): on `D(f)` the element `f` already maps
700	to a unit of the structure ring `Γ(O_{Spec R}, D(f))` (the away-localization
701	`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`), and the `R`-action on `Γ(M, D(f))` factors through it via

712	/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a
713	sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` together with quasi-coherent data `q` (a — possibly
714	infinite — open cover `q.X : q.I → (Spec R).Opens` of `⊤` with a presentation of

716	whose basic opens cover `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), with each basic open `D(r)`
717	(`r ∈ t`) contained in some member `q.X i` of the presentation cover.

720	quasi-compactness of `Spec R` plus the basic-open basis (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`)

722	`Opens.grothendieckTopology` sieve via `Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff`, and finiteness is extracted
723	through `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. To obtain `q` from `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`, take

730	theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
731	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
732	    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
733	      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
734	  classical
735	  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
736	  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
737	    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
738	    rw [eq_top_iff]
739	    intro x _
740	    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
741	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
742	    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
743	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
744	    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
745	    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
746	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi
747	    exact ⟨r, ⟨i, hVle⟩, hxV⟩
748	  obtain ⟨t, htsub, htspan⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite G).mp hspanG
749	  exact ⟨t, htspan, fun r hr => htsub hr⟩

751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence

1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is

1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are

1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1

1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))

1103	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1104	
1105	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1106	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1107	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1108	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1109	quasi-coherence datum.

1111	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1112	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1113	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via

1116	equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
1117	`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
1118	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
1119	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
1120	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1121	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/

1189	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base

1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _

1214	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by

1228	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by

1315	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D: the section-localization descent
1316	
1317	The keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` reduces (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks
1318	`lemma-invert-f-sections`) to a finite-cover sheaf-gluing argument.  The single geometric input
1319	is the **per-cover-element** fact that on each `D(r)` of a finite cover `{D(r_j)}` of `Spec R`
1320	(with `D(r_j) ≤ q.X i`), the basic-open restriction `Γ(M, D(r)) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ D(r))` is a
1321	localization at `powers f` — this is exactly the P1 local-tilde data transported to sections, and
1322	is the gated hypothesis `Hfr` below.  Given `Hfr` (for every `r` whose `D(r)` sits inside a cover
1323	member, hence also for the overlaps `D(r r')`), the descent is pure sheaf theory: separatedness
1324	gives the `exists_of_eq` field, gluing the patched compatible family gives `surj'`, and the global
1325	`map_units` field holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`). -/
1326	
1327	/-- A finite family `t` spanning `R` gives a basic-open cover of `Spec R`: the supremum of the
1328	`D(r)` over `r ∈ t` is `⊤`.  Project-local glue feeding the sheaf-gluing reduction of the
1329	section-localization descent. -/
1330	private lemma iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {t : Finset R}
1331	    (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤) :
1332	    (⨆ r : {x // x ∈ t}, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r : R) : (Spec R).Opens)) = ⊤ := by
1333	  rw [iSup_subtype]
1334	  have h := (PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff' (s := (t : Set R))).mpr hspan
1335	  simpa using h

1337	/-- Restriction maps of `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M` compose: restricting `A → B → C` equals the
1338	direct restriction `A → C`.  Poset-hom uniqueness makes the two intermediate morphisms compose to
1339	the direct one.  Project-local bookkeeping for the section-localization descent. -/
1340	private lemma res_comp {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1341	    (F : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} ↑R) ↑(Spec R).toPresheafedSpace)
1342	    {A B C : (Spec R).Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B) (hCA : C ≤ A)

1663	/-- **(I) Ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport.** Given a ring isomorphism
1664	`σ : R ≃+* R'`, two `σ`-semilinear additive isomorphisms `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`, `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` (i.e.
1665	`eᵢ (a • x) = σ a • eᵢ x`), and an `R'`-linear map `h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂` intertwining a localization
1666	map `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the map `h` is a localization at the image submonoid
1667	`S.map σ`. Mathlib only provides the same-ring `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`; this crosses a
1668	ring iso, the form needed to turn the `Ab`/semilinear section iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` into the
1669	`R`-linear `Hfr` data. Project-local. -/
1670	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear
1671	    {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] (σ : R ≃+* R')
1672	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
1673	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]
1674	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module R' N₁] [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module R' N₂]
1675	    (S : Submonoid R)
1676	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule S g]
1677	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
1678	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
1679	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
1680	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂)
1681	    (hh : ∀ x, h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)) :
1682	    IsLocalizedModule (S.map (σ : R →+* R')) h where
1683	  map_units x := by
1684	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hsx⟩ := x.2
1685	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1686	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨s, hs⟩
1687	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
1688	    have hfun : (⇑(algebraMap R' (Module.End R' N₂) (↑x : R')))
1689	        = ⇑e₂ ∘ ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (⟨s, hs⟩ : S)) ∘ ⇑e₂.symm := by
1690	      funext y
1691	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply,
1692	        Module.algebraMap_end_apply, he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply]
1693	      congr 1
1694	      exact hsx.symm
1695	    rw [hfun]
1696	    exact e₂.bijective.comp (hsrc.comp e₂.symm.bijective)
1697	  surj y := by
1698	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S g (e₂.symm y)
1699	    refine ⟨⟨e₁ x, ⟨σ ↑s, ↑s, s.2, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1700	    have he : e₂ ((↑s : R) • e₂.symm y) = e₂ (g x) := congrArg e₂ hx
1701	    rw [he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply, ← hh] at he
1702	    exact he
1703	  exists_of_eq {y₁ y₂} heq := by
1704	    have h1 : e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₁)) = e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₂)) := by
1705	      rw [← hh, ← hh, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply]; exact heq
1706	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S) (f := g) (e₂.injective h1)
1707	    refine ⟨⟨σ ↑c, ↑c, c.2, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
1708	    have hc' : (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₁ = (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₂ := hc
1709	    have hcc := congrArg e₁ hc'
1710	    rw [he₁, he₁, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply] at hcc
1711	    exact hcc

1713	/-- **(II) Localization at a base-changed submonoid descends to the base ring.** If `g` is
1714	`Rr`-linear and exhibits a localization at `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring
1715	`Rr` (an algebra over `R` — here `R` localized at some `r`), then its restriction of scalars to `R`
1716	is a localization
1717	at `powers f` over `R`. This lets the `R_r`-level localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces
1718	on the slice `Spec R_r` be read back as the `R`-level `Hfr` data the cover-form descent consumes.

1720	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap
1721	    {R Rr : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Rr] [Algebra R Rr]
1722	    {M₁ M₂ : Type*} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [Module Rr M₁] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₁]
1723	    [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂] [Module Rr M₂] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₂]
1724	    (f : R) (g : M₁ →ₗ[Rr] M₂)
1725	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g] :
1726	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (g.restrictScalars R) where
1727	  map_units x := by
1728	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
1729	    have hn' : f ^ n = ↑x := hn
1730	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1731	    have hmem : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n ∈ Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f) := ⟨n, rfl⟩
1732	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨_, hmem⟩
1733	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
1734	    have hfun : ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (↑x : R))
1735	        = ⇑(algebraMap Rr (Module.End Rr M₂) ((algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n)) := by
1736	      funext z
1737	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, ← hn', ← map_pow,
1738	        algebraMap_smul]
1739	    rw [hfun]; exact hsrc
1740	  surj y := by

1742	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1743	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑s := hn
1744	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1745	    have hsmul : (f ^ n : R) • y = (↑s : Rr) • y := by
1746	      rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
1747	    change (f ^ n : R) • y = (g.restrictScalars R) x
1748	    rw [hsmul, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars]; exact hx
1749	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} heq := by
1750	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f))
1751	      (f := g) heq
1752	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
1753	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑c := hn
1754	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
1755	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
1756	    have e2 : (f ^ n : R) • x₂ = (↑c : Rr) • x₂ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
1757	    change (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (f ^ n : R) • x₂
1758	    rw [e1, e2]; exact hc

1760	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: pullback-section transport
1761	
1762	The remaining gated ingredient of the named gap1 keystone
1763	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` is the **section-level** analogue of P1's object-level
1764	transport: for an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and a sheaf of modules `M` on `Y`, the sections of the
1765	geometric pullback `(pullback f).obj M` over an open `U ⊆ X` are canonically identified with the
1766	sections of `M` over the image `f ''ᵁ U`.

1769	(`restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`) at `M`, using the *definitional* identity
1770	`Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`, `rfl`). Because
1771	both sides are `Γ(-, U)` of a single fixed module isomorphism, naturality in `U` (intertwining the
1772	presheaf restriction maps) is free: it is the naturality of the underlying abelian-presheaf
1773	morphism. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
1774	
1775	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the image**
1776	(gap1, Hfr section transport, general open). For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, a sheaf of modules
1777	`M` on `Y`, and an open `U ⊆ X`, the additive groups of sections satisfy
1778	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. This is `Γ(-, U)` of `(restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)⁻¹`
1779	at `M`; the codomain is `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` definitionally. Naturality
1780	in `U` is `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality`. -/
1781	noncomputable def gammaPullbackImageIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
1782	    (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
1783	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) :=
1784	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op U)).mapIso
1785	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)
1786

1790	naturality of the underlying morphism of abelian presheaves. -/
1791	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
1792	    (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :

1795	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf).naturality i.op

1802	    (M : Y.Modules) :

1130	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data and scheme
1131	
1132	Assemble the affine charts, principal-open overlaps, transition isomorphisms, and the
1133	cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1134	`{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`; its `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)`

1137	/-- The **Grassmannian glue data** (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the `Scheme.GlueData` whose
1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued

1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map

1482	theorem valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) :
1483	    ValuativeCriterion.Uniqueness (toSpecZ d r) := by
1484	  haveI : IsSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := isSeparatedToSpecZ d r
1485	  exact IsSeparated.valuativeCriterion (toSpecZ d r)

1487	/-- **The minor-ratio identity** `θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_{K'}) · P^I_J = P^I_{K'}` over `R^I_J`
1488	(generalises `transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul`, the `K' = I` case where `P^I_I = 1`): the
1489	pre-localisation transition hom sends the `K'`-minor of `X^J` to `det((X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_{K'}) =
1490	P^I_{K'} / P^I_J`. This is the algebraic core of the valuative-criterion existence argument
1491	(Nitsure §1): pulling back through a `K`-point `f : R^I → K` it yields
1492	`g(P^J_{K'}) = f(P^I_{K'}) / f(P^I_J)`, whose valuation `≥ 0` (Nitsure additive) / `≤ 1`
1493	(multiplicative) by minimality drives the entries of `g(X^J)` into the valuation ring.
1494	Project-local: feeds the existence half of `ValuativeCriterion (toSpecZ d r)`. -/
1495	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1496	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1497	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) *
1498	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1499	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1500	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1501	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1502	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet]
1503	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
1504	        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))
1505	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
1506	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
1507	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
1508	  rw [hsub, Matrix.det_mul]
1509	  have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
1510	          (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).submatrix id
1511	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det
1512	        = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1513	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]; exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1514	  rw [hdet]
1515	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1516	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1517	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1518	  have hone : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det *
1519	      algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1520	    rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
1521	  rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm _ (algebraMap _ _ (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)), ← mul_assoc, hone, one_mul]

1523	/-- **Properness reduced to the existence part of the valuative criterion** (`lem:gr_proper`,
1524	reduction form): the structure morphism `toSpecZ : Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is proper as soon as the
1525	*existence* part of the valuative criterion holds. The three "cheap" ingredients —
1526	`QuasiCompact` (`quasiCompact_toSpecZ`), `QuasiSeparated` (`quasiSeparated_toSpecZ`),
1527	`LocallyOfFiniteType` (`locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ`) — and the *uniqueness* part
1528	(`valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ`, free from separatedness) are discharged here, so the only
1529	remaining obligation for `Grassmannian.isProper` is the Nitsure chart-selection existence
1530	statement `ValuativeCriterion.Existence (toSpecZ d r)`. Project-local. -/
1531	theorem isProper_of_valuativeExistence (d r : ℕ)
1532	    (hE : ValuativeCriterion.Existence (toSpecZ d r)) : IsProper (toSpecZ d r) := by
1533	  haveI : QuasiCompact (toSpecZ d r) := quasiCompact_toSpecZ d r
1534	  haveI : QuasiSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := quasiSeparated_toSpecZ d r
1535	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (toSpecZ d r) := locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ d r
1536	  apply IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion
1537	  exact ValuativeCriterion.iff.mpr ⟨hE, valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ d r⟩

1539	/-! ## Existence step E1 — chart selection (`scaffold` GrassmannianCells.lean)
1540	
1541	The existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1, "Properness") is
1542	the genuine geometric content. Its first step (E1) is the chart-selection factorization:
1543	a `K`-point of `Gr(d,r)` for a field `K` factors through a single affine chart
1544	`U^I = Spec R^I`. Since `Spec K` is a single point and the chart immersions jointly cover
1545	`Gr(d,r)`, the image point lies in the range of some chart immersion, and a morphism out of
1546	`Spec K` whose image lies in the range of an open immersion factors through it
1547	(`IsOpenImmersion.lift`); affineness of the chart turns the lift into `Spec` of a ring map
1548	(`Spec.preimage`).
1549	
1550	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization`
1551	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1552	
1553	/-- **E1 — the `K`-point factors through a single chart**
1554	(`lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization`): for a field `K`, any morphism
1555	`i₁ : Spec K ⟶ Gr(d,r)` factors as `Spec(f) ≫ ι_I` through a single chart immersion `ι_I` of
1556	the glue datum (`theGlueData`), for some size-`d` subset `I` and ring homomorphism
1557	`f : R^I = ℤ[X^I] → K`. Project-local: step E1 of the valuative-criterion existence argument
1558	(Nitsure §1, "Properness"). -/
1559	theorem existence_chart_factorization (d r : ℕ) {K : Type} [Field K]
1560	    (i₁ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ scheme d r) :
1561	    ∃ (I : (theGlueData d r).J)
1562	      (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K),
1563	      i₁ = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f) ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
1564	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty (↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K))) := inferInstance
1565	  obtain ⟨I, y, hy⟩ := (theGlueData d r).ι_jointly_surjective (i₁.base x₀)
1566	  have hrange : Set.range i₁.base ⊆ Set.range ((theGlueData d r).ι I).base := by
1567	    rintro z ⟨x', rfl⟩
1568	    rw [Subsingleton.elim x' x₀]
1569	    exact ⟨y, hy⟩
1570	  haveI hoi : IsOpenImmersion ((theGlueData d r).ι I) := (theGlueData d r).ι_isOpenImmersion I
1571	  refine ⟨I, (Spec.preimage
1572	    (@IsOpenImmersion.lift _ _ _ ((theGlueData d r).ι I) i₁ hoi hrange)).hom, ?_⟩
1573	  rw [CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, Spec.map_preimage]
1574	  exact (@IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _ ((theGlueData d r).ι I) i₁ hoi hrange).symm
1575	
1576	/-- **E2 — minimal-valuation chart selection** (`lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation`):
1577	given a valuation ring `R` with fraction field `K`, valuation `v := ValuationRing.valuation R K`,
1578	a chart index `I` and a ring hom `f : R^I → K`, there is a chart index `J` *maximising*
1579	`v (f (P^I_J))` over the finite index set of size-`d` subsets, and at the maximiser
1580	`f (P^I_J) ≠ 0` (so the matrix `f(X^I_J)` is invertible over `K`). Since `P^I_I = 1`
1581	(`minorDet_self`) the value at `I` is `v 1 = 1`, so the maximum is `≥ 1 > 0`. Project-local:
1582	step E2 of the valuative-criterion existence argument (Nitsure §1, "Properness"). -/
1583	theorem existence_minimal_valuation (d r : ℕ)
1584	    {R K : Type} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] [ValuationRing R] [Field K]
1585	    [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K]
1586	    (I : (theGlueData d r).J)
1587	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K) :
1588	    ∃ J : (theGlueData d r).J,
1589	      (∀ J' : (theGlueData d r).J,
1590	        ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2))
1591	          ≤ ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)))
1592	      ∧ f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) ≠ 0 := by
1593	  haveI : Nonempty (theGlueData d r).J := ⟨I⟩
1594	  haveI : Finite (theGlueData d r).J :=
1595	    inferInstanceAs (Finite {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d})
1596	  set v := ValuationRing.valuation R K with hv
1597	  obtain ⟨J, hJmax⟩ := Finite.exists_max
1598	    (fun J' : (theGlueData d r).J => v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2)))
1599	  refine ⟨J, hJmax, ?_⟩
1600	  have h1 : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 I.1 I.2 I.2)) = 1 := by
1601	    rw [minorDet_self d r I.1 I.2, map_one, Valuation.map_one]
1602	  have hle : (1 : ValuationRing.ValueGroup R K) ≤ v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := by
1603	    have := hJmax I; rwa [h1] at this
1604	  have hne : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) ≠ 0 := by
1605	    intro h; rw [h] at hle; exact (not_le.mpr zero_lt_one) hle
1606	  exact (Valuation.ne_zero_iff v).mp hne

1608	/-- **E3 ratio core — the pulled-back minor-ratio identity**
1609	(displayed equation of `lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`): for a ring hom
1610	`f : R^I → F` under which the minor `P^I_J` is a unit, let `f' : R^I_J → F` be its
1611	away-localisation lift (`IsLocalization.Away.lift`). Then `g := f' ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` satisfies, for
1612	every third subset `K`,
1613	`g(P^J_K) · f(P^I_J) = f(P^I_K)`, i.e. `g(P^J_K) = f(P^I_K) / f(P^I_J)`.
1614	This is `f'` applied to the ring-level minor-ratio identity `transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul`.
1615	Project-local: the algebraic core of step E3; together with the valuation bound from E2 it
1616	drives the entries of `g(X^J)` into the valuation ring. (The remaining step — that every free
1617	entry `x^J_{p,q}` is, up to sign, such a minor `P^J_K` via cofactor expansion of a
1618	column-substituted identity — is the one matrix-algebra gap still open for E3.) -/
1619	theorem existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
1620	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) {F : Type} [CommRing F]
1621	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ →+* F)
1622	    (hf : IsUnit (f (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) :
1623	    (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) hf)
1624	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))
1625	        * f (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1626	      = f (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1627	  have h := congrArg (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) hf)
1628	    (transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1629	  rwa [map_mul, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq] at h

1631	/-! ## Existence step E3 — cofactor expansion (`scaffold` GrassmannianCells.lean)
1632	
1633	The remaining matrix-algebra gap of `lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`:
1634	each free entry `x^J_{p,q}` of the universal matrix `X^J` (`q ∉ J`) equals, up to sign,
1635	the minor `P^J_{K'}` with `K' = (J \ {j_p}) ∪ {q}` — the determinant obtained by replacing
1636	the `p`-th identity column of `X^J_J` by column `q`. Cofactor expansion of a column-substituted
1637	identity matrix: `det((1).updateCol p v) = v p`.
1638	
1639	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`
1640	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1641	
1642	/-- The determinant of the identity matrix with column `p` replaced by the vector `v`
1643	is the `p`-th entry `v p` (cofactor expansion along the substituted column). Project-local:
1644	Mathlib has `Matrix.cramer_apply` but no direct `det (1.updateCol …)` lemma. -/
1645	private lemma det_one_updateCol {d : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
1646	    (p : Fin d) (v : Fin d → R) :
1647	    ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R).updateCol p v).det = v p := by
1648	  rw [← Matrix.cramer_apply]
1649	  have h : (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R).cramer v = v := by
1650	    have := Matrix.mulVec_cramer (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) v
1651	    rwa [Matrix.one_mulVec, Matrix.det_one, one_smul] at this
1652	  rw [h]
1653	
1654	/-- **Cofactor expansion of a free entry as a signed minor**: for `q ∉ J` and a row index
1655	`p`, the free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}` of the universal matrix `X^J` equals, up to a sign,
1656	the minor `P^J_{K'}` where `K' = insert q (J.erase j_p)` (replace the `p`-th column `j_p` of
1657	`X^J_J` by column `q`). Since `X^J_J` is the identity, the column-substituted minor is the
1658	determinant of `(1).updateCol p (column q)`, namely `x^J_{p,q}`, up to the sign of the
1659	column-reindexing permutation. Project-local: the matrix-algebra core of step E3
1660	(`lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`). -/
1661	theorem exists_minorDet_eq_free_entry (d r : ℕ) (J : Finset (Fin r)) (hJ : J.card = d)
1662	    (p : Fin d) (q : Fin r) (hq : q ∉ J) :
1663	    ∃ (K' : Finset (Fin r)) (hK' : K'.card = d),
1664	      minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' = MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩) ∨
1665	      minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' = - MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩) := by
1666	  classical
1667	  set jp : Fin r := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) with hjp
1668	  have hjpJ : jp ∈ J := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p).2
1669	  set K' : Finset (Fin r) := insert q (J.erase jp) with hK'def
1670	  have hqnoterase : q ∉ J.erase jp := fun h => hq (Finset.mem_of_mem_erase h)
1671	  have hd1 : 1 ≤ d := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero (fun h => by subst h; exact Fin.elim0 p)
1672	  have hK' : K'.card = d := by
1673	    rw [hK'def, Finset.card_insert_of_notMem hqnoterase, Finset.card_erase_of_mem hjpJ, hJ]
1674	    omega
1675	  refine ⟨K', hK', ?_⟩
1676	  -- Injectivity of the order embedding `Fin d → Fin r` through `J`.
1677	  have hJinj : ∀ k k' : Fin d,
1678	      (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k' : Fin r) ↔ k = k' := by
1679	    intro k k'; rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
1680	  -- The column-index map used to read off the substituted-identity minor.
1681	  set colMap : Fin d → Fin r :=
1682	    fun k => if k = p then q else (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) with hcm
1683	  -- `colMap k ∈ K'`.
1684	  have hmem : ∀ k, colMap k ∈ K' := by
1685	    intro k
1686	    simp only [hcm]
1687	    by_cases hk : k = p
1688	    · rw [if_pos hk, hK'def]; exact Finset.mem_insert_self _ _
1689	    · rw [if_neg hk, hK'def, Finset.mem_insert]
1690	      refine Or.inr ?_
1691	      rw [Finset.mem_erase]
1692	      refine ⟨?_, (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2⟩
1693	      rw [hjp]; intro hcontra
1694	      exact hk ((hJinj k p).mp hcontra)
1695	  -- Injectivity of `colMap`.
1696	  have hcmInj : Function.Injective colMap := by
1697	    intro k k' hkk'
1698	    simp only [hcm] at hkk'
1699	    by_cases hk : k = p <;> by_cases hk' : k' = p
1700	    · rw [hk, hk']
1701	    · exfalso
1702	      rw [if_pos hk, if_neg hk'] at hkk'
1703	      exact hq (hkk' ▸ (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k').2)
1704	    · exfalso
1705	      rw [if_neg hk, if_pos hk'] at hkk'
1706	      exact hq (hkk'.symm ▸ (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2)
1707	    · rw [if_neg hk, if_neg hk'] at hkk'
1708	      exact (hJinj k k').mp hkk'
1709	  -- The order-reindexing permutation `σ` with `oiK' ∘ σ = colMap`.
1710	  set oiK' : Fin d → Fin r := fun k => (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK' k : Fin r) with hoiK'
1711	  set σfun : Fin d → Fin d :=
1712	    fun k => (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK').symm ⟨colMap k, hmem k⟩ with hσfun
1713	  have hσInj : Function.Injective σfun := by
1714	    intro k k' h
1715	    simp only [hσfun] at h
1716	    have h2 : (⟨colMap k, hmem k⟩ : ↥K') = ⟨colMap k', hmem k'⟩ :=
1717	      (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK').symm.injective h
1718	    exact hcmInj (Subtype.mk_eq_mk.mp h2)
1719	  set σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin d) :=
1720	    Equiv.ofBijective σfun (Finite.injective_iff_bijective.mp hσInj) with hσ
1721	  have hσprop : ∀ k, oiK' (σ k) = colMap k := by
1722	    intro k
1723	    simp only [hoiK', hσ, Equiv.ofBijective_apply, hσfun]
1724	    rw [OrderIso.apply_symm_apply]
1725	  -- The substituted-identity matrix and its determinant.
1726	  set v : Fin d → MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ :=
1727	    fun k' => universalMatrix d r J hJ k' q with hv
1728	  have hBupd : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap
1729	      = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d)
1730	          (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)).updateCol p v := by
1731	    apply Matrix.ext
1732	    intro k' k
1733	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, Matrix.updateCol_apply]
1734	    simp only [hcm]
1735	    by_cases hk : k = p
1736	    · simp only [if_pos hk, hv]
1737	    · simp only [if_neg hk, universalMatrix]
1738	      rw [dif_pos (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2, Matrix.one_apply]
1739	      by_cases hkk : k' = k
1740	      · rw [if_pos ((hJinj k' k).mpr hkk), if_pos hkk]
1741	      · rw [if_neg (fun h => hkk ((hJinj k' k).mp h)), if_neg hkk]
1742	  have hdetB : ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap).det
1743	      = MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩) := by
1744	    rw [hBupd, det_one_updateCol]
1745	    simp only [hv, universalMatrix, dif_neg hq]
1746	  -- Relate `colMap` to the `K'`-minor via the permutation `σ`.

1748	  have hsubEq : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap
1749	      = ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id oiK').submatrix id (σ : Fin d → Fin d) := by
1750	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_submatrix, Function.comp_id, hcolEq]

1752	    rw [minorDet]
1753	  have hsign : MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩)
1754	      = (↑(↑(Equiv.Perm.sign σ) : ℤ) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
1755	        * minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' := by
1756	    rw [← hdetB, hsubEq, Matrix.det_permute', hminor]
1757	  rcases Int.units_eq_one_or (Equiv.Perm.sign σ) with hs | hs
1758	  · left
1759	    rw [hsign, hs]; simp
1760	  · right
1761	    rw [hsign, hs]; simp
1762	
1763	/-- **E3 — entries land in `R`; `g` factors through the valuation ring**
1764	(`lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`): for a valuation ring `R` with fraction
1765	field `K`, a chart index `I` with a `K`-point `f : R^I → K`, and the minimal-valuation chart
1766	index `J` (so `f(P^I_J)` is a unit and `v(f(P^I_{J'})) ≤ v(f(P^I_J))` for all `J'`), the
1767	composite `g := f' ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` (where `f' = lift f along P^I_J`) sends every element of
1768	`R^J = ℤ[X^J]` into the subring `(algebraMap R K).range ⊆ K`. Hence `g` factors through `R`.
1769	
1770	Proof: by `MvPolynomial` induction it suffices to check the constants (in `ℤ ⊆ R`) and the
1771	free generators `x^J_{p,q}`. Each generator equals `± P^J_{K'}` (`exists_minorDet_eq_free_entry`),
1772	and `g(P^J_{K'}) = f(P^I_{K'})/f(P^I_J)` has valuation `≤ 1` by the maximality of `J`, hence lies
1773	in `R = v.integer` (`ValuationRing.range_algebraMap_eq`). Project-local: the genuine geometric
1774	content of the existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1). -/
1775	theorem existence_factor_through_valuationRing (d r : ℕ)
